task:
Phase 3, Atom A — independent adversarial cross-doc re-verification of the already-reconciled documentation surface. Phase 1+2 reconciled ARCHITECTURE.md, ADRs, all docs/, README/CONTRIBUTING, and cocoder/ governance per-file. Your job is a FRESH, skeptical pass that hunts what the per-file audits missed: (1) cross-document inconsistencies — the same claim stated two ways in two files, contradictory descriptions of the same subsystem/flow/command across ARCHITECTURE.md vs docs/ vs ADRs; (2) spot-check that corrected claims still trace to LIVE code by file/path evidence — pick the load-bearing claims in ARCHITECTURE.md (architecture, run model, commit spine, write-scope/commit-gate gating, persona roles) and grep the actual packages/ code to confirm each; (3) any stale concrete refs (file paths, CLI commands/flags, ADR ids) that no longer resolve.

Deliverable: a discrepancy inventory written to docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md. Each row MUST carry: the claim + its file:line, a verdict (CONSISTENT / DOC-WRONG / CODE-WRONG / STALE-REF), and a resolution. For DOC-WRONG and STALE-REF discrepancies that fall inside YOUR write scope (docs/**, ARCHITECTURE.md), FIX them in place and cite the live-code path that proves the correct claim. For anything that would require editing cocoder/** governance, or any CODE-WRONG conflict (doc is right, code is wrong), DO NOT edit — record it in the inventory flagged 'OSCAR-LANE: cocoder/ governance edit' or 'CODE-CONFLICT: needs ticket/founder decision' so Oscar can route it.

Acceptance criteria: docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md exists with a complete inventory; zero rows left without a verdict+resolution; every doc-side fix you made cites a real file/path; spot-checked claims name the live code file confirming them. Must not break: do not edit cocoder/** (outside your scope), do not weaken or delete the existing worklists docs/architecture-truth-audit.md or docs/docs-files-truth-audit.md, and touch only documentation (no code changes — this is a doc-truth pass). Report the count of discrepancies by verdict category when done.

Phase 3, Atom B — audit the un-audited NORMATIVE surface for stale CONCRETE references. Phase 1/2 focused on code-describing docs; this atom covers the base persona/standards/plays and the workspace deltas, which describe HOW agents operate and can drift just as badly.

Surface to audit (read every file):
  1. packages/personas/base/** — base personas (oz/oscar/bob/deb/quinn .md), base standards (packages/personas/base/standards/**), and base plays (packages/personas/base/plays/**).
  2. cocoder/personas/deltas/** and cocoder/standards/** — the workspace-specific deltas/extensions.

What to check in each file — stale CONCRETE refs only (not prose/opinion):
  - File/dir PATHS that no longer resolve (test -e / find the real path).
  - CLI commands and FLAGS that no longer exist or were renamed (grep the live cli/daemon source: packages/cli/src/**, packages/daemon/src/** — e.g. cocoder subcommands, oz tool names, flags like --strict-dirt, commit-support/teardown/request-deb-repair invocations).
  - ADR ids / ADR filenames referenced that have been renumbered or renamed (cross-check against cocoder/decisions/**; remember 0010 is taxonomy-and-authoring, 0013 is orchestration-observation — old 'objective-first'/'multi-atom-delegation' names are stale).

Write scope and the split (IMPORTANT):
  - packages/personas/base/** IS your write scope and this is a verified run, so FIX stale concrete refs there IN PLACE — BUT only where the corrected ref stays portable and role-neutral (ADR-0012). A base file must teach the role with repo-specific nouns stripped out: if you find a base file containing a repo-specific concrete ref that should not be in base at all (a cocoder/-specific path, a workspace-only command), do NOT silently 'fix' the path — FLAG it as an ADR-0012 portability violation to re-scope into the workspace delta, and leave the base file for that decision.
  - cocoder/personas/deltas/** and cocoder/standards/** are OUTSIDE your write scope (they route to Deb). Do NOT edit them. RECORD every stale ref you find there in the inventory, flagged 'DEB-LANE: cocoder/ delta edit'.

Tests (mandatory — base governance is test-pinned): persona/standards/play content is asserted by suites. After any packages/personas/base/** edit, run the affected persona/Play tests (e.g. the suites under packages/core/tests that load persona prompts/plays, and packages/personas tests if present) and report the actual command + pass counts. A base edit is not green until its suite passes — do not assert green from typecheck alone.

Deliverable: append an 'Atom B — normative surface' section to docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md with one row per finding: file:line, the stale ref, verdict (STALE-PATH / STALE-CLI / STALE-ADR-ID / PORTABILITY-VIOLATION / CONSISTENT-SPOTCHECK), evidence (the failing test -e / grep / ADR cross-check), and resolution (fixed-in-base with the corrected ref, or DEB-LANE/flag). Report counts by verdict and the list of base files you edited.

Acceptance: inventory section exists and is complete; every base-file fix cites the live path/command/ADR that proves the correction and is portable; no cocoder/** or cocoder/standards/** or cocoder/personas/deltas/** edits; affected persona/Play suite run green with command+counts shown; no code-behavior changes (refs/text only).

Phase 3, Atom C — clarity / elegance pass over the reconciled documentation (Objective criterion 3: docs read clear, correct, and stale-reference-free). This is NOT a re-truth pass (Atoms A/B covered truth) and NOT license to rewrite for taste. Make only changes that demonstrably improve clarity or remove dead weight WITHOUT changing any factual claim.

Scope (your write lane only): ARCHITECTURE.md and docs/** (the surface reconciled in Phase 1/2/Atom A). Do NOT touch cocoder/**, packages/**, or scripts/**.

Look for and fix exactly these three classes:
  1. ONE-OWNER VIOLATIONS / duplication — the same rule, definition, or claim stated in full in two+ governed docs where one file should own it and the others should link to it (elegance standard: one owner per concept). When you find one, keep the authoritative owner, replace the duplicate copies with a one-line pointer/link to the owner, and confirm the link resolves. Do NOT delete a claim that has no other home. For each dedup, name the owner you kept.
  2. STALE / DEAD REFERENCES — leftover references to removed concepts (e.g. 'developer mode', run branches, pending-landing, integration-verify), dead relative links, or pointers to files that moved. Fix the link or remove the dangling reference. Verify each corrected link target exists (test -e / resolve the path).
  3. AWKWARD RESIDUE FROM THE ATOM-A REWRITES — the commit-spine paragraphs in ARCHITECTURE.md (around the 'one mode' / commitOnlyScope rewrite), docs/glossary.md write-scope entry, and docs/fault-injection-live-proofs.md were edited mid-prose in Atom A; read them cold and smooth only genuinely clumsy wording. Preserve every fact and every cited path exactly.

Hard constraints: change NO factual claim, add NO new claim, and keep every file:line/path citation that already exists. If you are unsure whether something is a clarity fix or a truth change, treat it as out of scope and instead RECORD it (don't edit) in a new 'Atom C - clarity/elegance' section of docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md. That section must also list every dedup you made (concept + owner kept + files pointed at it) and every stale/dead reference you fixed, with before/after.

Acceptance: docs/phase3-cross-doc-reverification.md has an 'Atom C' section enumerating each change (dedup owner, fixed reference, or smoothed passage); every link you touched resolves; no factual claim changed (a reviewer can diff and see only wording/link/structure deltas, never a changed assertion); no edits outside ARCHITECTURE.md / docs/**. Report the count of dedups, fixed references, and smoothed passages.
